update.headline: The Artemis II mission is successfully on a free-return trajectory and the crew has passed the equidistance point between Earth and the Moon.
details: Orion spacecraft is in excellent health with all primary systems performing within nominal parameters.
A minor odor reported in the hygiene bay was traced to insulation and is not a safety concern.
The crew crossed the halfway point of their journey on April 4 and are now closer to the Moon than Earth.
Flight controllers successfully canceled the first planned outbound trajectory correction due to the high precision of the TLI burn.
update.crewStatus: The four-person crew is in high spirits, in good physical condition, and maintaining a routine of cardiovascular exercise and medical training.
update.lastEvent: Crew crossed the halfway point of the mission journey on April 4, 2026.

milestones.label: Launch
milestones.detail: SLS liftoff from Pad 39B

milestones.label: Core Stage Separation
milestones.detail: MECO & core stage jettison

milestones.label: Solar Array Deploy
milestones.detail: Four Orion solar wings extended

milestones.label: Perigee Raise
milestones.detail: Orion performed perigee raise and apogee raise burns.

milestones.label: Proximity Ops
milestones.detail: Crew successfully demonstrated manual piloting of Orion near the ICPS upper stage.

milestones.label: Translunar Injection
milestones.detail: 5-minute, 50-second burn increased velocity by 1,274 fps to break Earth orbit.

milestones.label: ICPS Separation
milestones.detail: Upper stage jettisoned

milestones.label: AVATAR Scientific Payload Checkout
milestones.detail: Testing 'organ-on-a-chip' technology to study deep space radiation effects on bone marrow cells.

milestones.label: Earth Photography By Crew
milestones.detail: Crew captured images of Earth's auroras and zodiacal light.

milestones.label: Deep Space Cpr Demo
milestones.detail: Crew successfully tested medical emergency protocols and CPR techniques in microgravity.

milestones.label: Outbound Trajectory Correction (OTC-1)
milestones.detail: Burn canceled by Mission Control as the current trajectory requires no further adjustment.

milestones.label: Hygiene Bay Inspection
milestones.detail: Crew identified a faint burning smell in the hygiene bay, determined to be harmless insulation outgassing.

milestones.label: Lunar Distance Equalization
milestones.detail: Spacecraft crossed the point of equidistance between the Earth and the Moon.

milestones.label: CSA Live Downlink
milestones.detail: Live broadcast event featuring images of the lunar surface visible from the Orion spacecraft.

milestones.label: Female Distance Record
milestones.detail: Christina Koch officially surpassed the 70,000-mile mark, becoming the farthest woman from Earth.

milestones.label: Lunar Sphere Of Influence
milestones.detail: Entry into the Moon's gravitational sphere of influence.

milestones.label: Human Distance Record
milestones.detail: Crew expected to surpass the Apollo 13 record distance from Earth.

milestones.label: Farthest Distance from Earth Record
milestones.detail: Orion surpasses the maximum distance from Earth of previous Apollo missions.

milestones.label: Lunar Surface Observation
milestones.detail: Crew performs visual observations and photography of the lunar surface.

milestones.label: Communications Blackout
milestones.detail: Expected loss of signal as Orion passes behind the lunar far side.

milestones.label: Lunar Flyby
milestones.detail: Planned pass 4,700 miles above the lunar far side; first humans to see it since 1972.

milestones.label: Human Distance Record
milestones.detail: Expected to reach 252,799 miles from Earth, breaking the Apollo 13 record.

milestones.label: Earthrise Photo Ops
milestones.detail: Crew photographs Earthrise

milestones.label: ISS Crew Conversation
milestones.detail: Scheduled outreach event with crew members currently aboard the International Space Station.

milestones.label: Return Coast
milestones.detail: Free-return trajectory to Earth

milestones.label: Splashdown
milestones.detail: Final splashdown in the Pacific Ocean off the coast of San Diego.

milestones.label: Re-entry
milestones.detail: Skip re-entry at ~5,000°F

history.headline: The Artemis II mission is successfully on a free-return trajectory and the crew has passed the equidistance point between Earth and the Moon.
details: Orion spacecraft is in excellent health with all primary systems performing within nominal parameters.
A minor odor reported in the hygiene bay was traced to insulation and is not a safety concern.
The crew crossed the halfway point of their journey on April 4 and are now closer to the Moon than Earth.
Flight controllers successfully canceled the first planned outbound trajectory correction due to the high precision of the TLI burn.
history.crewStatus: The four-person crew is in high spirits, in good physical condition, and maintaining a routine of cardiovascular exercise and medical training.
history.lastEvent: Crew crossed the halfway point of the mission journey on April 4, 2026.
history.nextEvent: Lunar Flyby on April 6, 2026.

history.headline: Artemis II Orion spacecraft reaches equidistance point between Earth and the Moon on Flight Day 4.
details: The first Outbound Trajectory Correction (OTC-1) burn was canceled because the spacecraft's path following TLI is exceptionally precise.
Mission Specialist Christina Koch officially became the farthest woman from Earth, surpassing the 70,000-mile mark.
The crew successfully addressed a brief 'burning smell' from the toilet heater insulation, which has been classified as a minor nuisance.
Astronauts are conducting medical demonstrations, including CPR in microgravity and health kit testing.
history.crewStatus: Commander Reid Wiseman, Pilot Victor Glover, and Mission Specialists Christina Koch and Jeremy Hansen are in high spirits and good health.
history.lastEvent: Reached the 'Equidistance Point' where Orion is now closer to the Moon than to Earth.
history.nextEvent: Lunar Flyby at an altitude of approximately 7,600 km from the surface.

history.headline: Orion spacecraft on precise trajectory to the Moon following high-precision TLI burn.
details: Mission controllers canceled the first trajectory correction burn (OTC-1) as Orion is maintaining a near-perfect course following the TLI burn.
The crew successfully managed a minor burning smell reported near the toilet hygiene bay, which was identified as non-hazardous insulation material.
Astronauts completed key medical and operational tests including a zero-gravity CPR demonstration and exercise equipment checkouts.
Orion has surpassed 245,000 km from Earth and is on track for a lunar flyby and record-breaking distance on April 6.
history.crewStatus: Reid Wiseman, Victor Glover, Christina Koch, and Jeremy Hansen are in high spirits and excellent health; currently focusing on photography and lunar approach prep.
history.lastEvent: Translunar Injection (TLI) engine burn completed April 2.
history.nextEvent: Lunar gravity assist flyby passing within 4,700 miles of the lunar surface on April 6.

history.headline: Orion spacecraft Integrity has passed the equidistance point between Earth and the Moon on Flight Day 3.
details: Orion is now closer to the Moon than to Earth, following a precise Translunar Injection burn that resulted in the cancellation of the first trajectory correction maneuver.
A minor technical issue involving an odor in the waste management system on Flight Day 2 was successfully resolved by the crew and ground teams.
Mission Specialist Christina Koch has officially set a new record for the farthest distance traveled from Earth by a woman.
The crew successfully completed zero-gravity medical drills including CPR rehearsals and routine physical health monitoring.
history.crewStatus: Commander Wiseman, Pilot Glover, and Specialists Koch and Hansen are in excellent health and high spirits, settling into deep-space routines.
history.lastEvent: Reached the halfway point between Earth and the Moon (Lunar Distance Equalization).
history.nextEvent: Entry into the Lunar Sphere of Influence (SOI) and closest lunar approach.

history.headline: Orion passes the halfway point to the Moon as the crew completes critical medical and communications checks.
details: The spacecraft has crossed the equidistance point between Earth and the Moon, now more than 229,000 km from Earth.
Flight controllers canceled the OTC-1 trajectory correction burn due to the high precision of the SLS and ESM during TLI.
The crew performed microgravity CPR demonstrations and medical kit checks to test emergency protocols in deep space.
Christina Koch successfully verified the emergency communications system via the Deep Space Network.
history.crewStatus: Commander Wiseman, Victor Glover, Christina Koch, and Jeremy Hansen are in excellent health and great spirits.
history.lastEvent: Passed the lunar equidistance point (halfway mark) at approximately 04:00 UTC.
history.nextEvent: Entry into the Lunar Sphere of Influence (SOI) late Sunday followed by the lunar flyby on Monday.

history.headline: The Artemis II crew has officially passed the lunar equidistance point and is now closer to the Moon than Earth.
details: The crew is in excellent health and high spirits while conducting deep-space medical and emergency protocol drills.
The Orion spacecraft is maintaining a precise free-return trajectory following an exceptionally accurate TLI burn.
A minor issue with the waste management system was successfully resolved by the crew earlier in the mission.
The mission is currently utilizing the Deep Space Network for communications, a first for a crewed mission in over 50 years.
sources: NASA Artemis II Mission Status Report
history.crewStatus: Excellent health and high spirits.
history.lastEvent: Lunar Distance Equalization achieved on April 4 at 04:00 UTC.
history.nextEvent: Lunar Sphere of Influence entry on April 5.

history.headline: Orion crosses the equidistant point and is now closer to the Moon than Earth.
details: NASA flight controllers canceled the OTC-1 burn because the Trans-Lunar Injection maneuver was precise enough to maintain course without adjustment.
The crew completed microgravity medical drills, rehearsing airway management and CPR procedures in deep space.
Orion surpassed the halfway point between Earth and the Moon at approximately 04:00 UTC on Mission Day 3.
Commander Wiseman and the crew are currently in a scheduled sleep period ahead of Mission Day 4 cabin reconfigurations.
history.crewStatus: Commander Wiseman, Glover, Koch, and Hansen are in excellent health and have completed deep-space photography and medical drills.
history.lastEvent: Crossing the equidistant point between Earth and the Moon.
history.nextEvent: Entry into the Lunar Sphere of Influence where lunar gravity becomes dominant.

history.headline: Orion surpasses the halfway point to the Moon on a precise trajectory toward a record-breaking lunar flyby.
details: Orion crossed the lunar distance equalization point at 04:00 UTC, now traveling closer to the Moon than Earth.
Mission control canceled the first outbound trajectory correction (OTC-1) due to the high accuracy of the TLI burn.
Crew successfully performed medical demonstrations in zero gravity, including CPR protocols and diagnostic tool testing.
Minor technical glitches involving the toilet, cabin alarms, and water dispenser have been successfully resolved or managed.
history.crewStatus: Wiseman, Glover, Koch, and Hansen are in great spirits and described as 'euphoric' following successful Flight Day 3 technical drills.
history.lastEvent: Crossed the 'equidistance' point between Earth and the Moon at 04:00 UTC.
history.nextEvent: Lunar observation training and live downlink event following the crew's current sleep period.

history.headline: Orion passes the gravitational halfway point to the Moon on Mission Day 3.
details: The Orion spacecraft is currently closer to the Moon than to Earth following a precise Trans-Lunar Injection burn.
The crew successfully completed a CPR medical demonstration and the first high-definition deep space laser communication downlink.
A minor issue with the waste management system (toilet) was successfully troubleshot and resolved by the crew earlier in the flight.
Orion remains on a free-return trajectory, ensuring a safe return to Earth using lunar gravity.
history.crewStatus: Wiseman, Glover, Koch, and Hansen are in excellent health and high spirits; currently in a scheduled rest period.
history.lastEvent: Passed the gravitational halfway point between Earth and the Moon at 04:00 UTC.
history.nextEvent: Entry into the Lunar Sphere of Influence (SOI) on April 5.

history.headline: Orion passes the lunar equidistance point as the crew enters the third day of flight.
details: The first Outbound Trajectory Correction (OTC-1) was cancelled as the initial TLI burn was precise enough to require no immediate adjustment.
Mission Specialist Christina Koch has officially broken the record for the farthest distance traveled from Earth by a woman.
Ground teams isolated a redundant helium branch after a reading anomaly, while a cabin leak warning was confirmed as a sensor glitch.
The crew successfully managed a minor plumbing issue and adjusted environmental systems to stabilize cabin temperatures.
history.crewStatus: The crew is in excellent health, performing space adaptation tests, CPR drills, and flywheel exercises.
history.lastEvent: Passed the equidistance point between Earth and the Moon at 04:00 UTC.
history.nextEvent: Lunar flyby and record-breaking maximum distance from Earth on April 6.

history.headline: Orion Integrity is more than halfway to the Moon after canceling its first course correction due to trajectory precision.
details: Mission control canceled the OTC-1 maneuver because the Translunar Injection burn was precise enough to maintain the required path.
The crew successfully resolved a minor technical issue with a waste management system fan, returning the system to full operation.
Orion has officially passed the halfway point and is now physically closer to the Moon than it is to the Earth.
The crew completed a live downlink with the Canadian Space Agency, sharing high-definition images of the Moon from the spacecraft hatch.
history.crewStatus: Commander Wiseman, Pilot Glover, and Specialists Koch and Hansen are in excellent health and performing regular flywheel exercises.
history.lastEvent: Official transition to being closer to the Moon than to the Earth occurred at 04:00 UTC.
history.nextEvent: Entry into the Lunar Sphere of Influence (SOI) and subsequent lunar flyby on April 6.

history.headline: Christina Koch sets record as the farthest woman to travel from Earth as Orion continues lunar transit.
details: Orion has surpassed 100,000 miles from Earth and is maintaining a high-precision trajectory toward the Moon.
Flight controllers cancelled the OTC-1 burn maneuver because the initial Translunar Injection was exceptionally accurate.
The crew successfully performed microgravity medical demonstrations, including CPR practice and diagnostic equipment testing.
Testing of the Orion Optical Communications system successfully transmitted high-definition video back to Earth.
sources: Artemis II Flight Status Report April 4 04:24 UTC
history.crewStatus: Commander Reid Wiseman, Victor Glover, Christina Koch, and Jeremy Hansen are in good health and high spirits.
history.lastEvent: Cancellation of the OTC-1 trajectory correction burn due to high mission precision.
history.nextEvent: Entry into the Lunar Sphere of Influence followed by lunar flyby and observation.

history.headline: Artemis II crew remains in good health as Orion continues its precise trajectory toward the lunar flyby.
details: Orion's trajectory is so precise that the first planned Outbound Trajectory Correction burn was cancelled.
The crew successfully performed medical demonstrations, including a microgravity CPR procedure test.
Minor issues with the waste management system and water dispenser have been resolved.
The crew is sharing high-resolution imagery of Earth from deep space.
history.crewStatus: The crew of four is in good health and high spirits.
history.lastEvent: Live CSA downlink event with Jeremy Hansen.
history.nextEvent: Lunar sphere of influence entry on April 5.

history.headline: Artemis II crew continues deep space transit toward the Moon with spacecraft systems performing as expected.
details: Mission control successfully canceled the first planned outbound trajectory correction burn due to precise navigation.
The four-person crew is in good health and adapting to the deep space environment.
Operations have focused on space adaptation, CPR demonstrations, and preparations for upcoming lunar observations.
Minor logistical adjustments are being managed regarding equipment and the waste management system.
history.crewStatus: The crew is healthy and successfully transitioning into deep-space operational routines.
history.lastEvent: Deep Space CPR Demo (completed April 3, 2026)
history.nextEvent: Lunar Sphere of Influence entry

history.headline: Artemis II passes 100,000 miles from Earth as trajectory precision cancels first correction burn.
details: Mission Control canceled the OTC-1 burn as Orion is already on a precise free-return trajectory.
Crew successfully performed a zero-gravity CPR demonstration and space adaptation tests.
Engineers and crew managed a potable water valve issue by transitioning to contingency storage bags.
The Universal Waste Management System is fully operational following successful plumbing repairs by the crew.
history.crewStatus: Crew is in excellent health and high spirits; currently performing photography and medical demos.
history.lastEvent: Translunar Injection (TLI) burn on April 2 successfully sent Orion toward the Moon.
history.nextEvent: Entry into the Lunar Sphere of Influence

history.headline: Artemis II trajectory precise enough to cancel scheduled correction burn on Flight Day 3.
details: Orion's trajectory following Translunar Injection was so accurate that mission control canceled the OTC-1 burn.
The crew performed a successful deep-space CPR medical demonstration and continues microgravity adaptation.
Technical issues with the Waste Management System and a false cabin leak indication have been fully resolved.
The spacecraft remains on a free-return trajectory that uses lunar gravity to swing back toward Earth.
sources: NASA Johnson Space Center Flight Control
history.crewStatus: Commander Wiseman, Pilot Glover, and Specialists Koch and Hansen are in excellent health and adapting well to deep space.
history.lastEvent: Completion of the Translunar Injection (TLI) burn, making the crew the first to leave Earth orbit since 1972.
history.nextEvent: Lunar closest approach at approximately 4,700 miles above the far side on April 6.

history.headline: Orion speeds toward the Moon on Flight Day 3 following successful Translunar Injection.
details: The crew of Orion Integrity completed a 5-minute 55-second TLI burn to reach escape velocity.
Mission activities include human health research (AVATAR study) and space adaptation exercises.
Engineers resolved minor issues with a toilet system controller and a water tank valve position.
Orion is on a free-return trajectory with a lunar flyby scheduled for April 6.
sources: Artemis II Flight Journal - Flight Day 3
history.crewStatus: Commander Wiseman, Pilot Glover, and Specialists Koch and Hansen are in excellent health and high spirits.
history.lastEvent: Successful completion of the Translunar Injection (TLI) burn at 23:49 UTC on April 2.
history.nextEvent: First Outbound Trajectory Correction (OTC-1) burn to refine Orion's path.

history.headline: Artemis II crew enters deep space for the first time in over 50 years after successful Translunar Injection.
details: Orion spacecraft 'Integrity' is transiting toward the Moon following a 5-minute 50-second TLI burn on April 2.
Crew is testing critical hardware including the flywheel exercise device and Orion Optical Communications System.
Minor issues with cabin pressure indications and waste management have been successfully resolved by the crew and ground teams.
The crew is currently preparing for the Outbound Trajectory Correction-1 (OTC-1) burn to refine their lunar approach.
sources: NASA Artemis II Mission Status Report
history.crewStatus: Commander Wiseman, Pilot Glover, and Specialists Koch and Hansen are in excellent health and spirits.
history.lastEvent: Completion of the Translunar Injection (TLI) burn on April 2 at 23:49Z.
history.nextEvent: Outbound Trajectory Correction-1 (OTC-1) burn scheduled for 22:49Z.

history.headline: Artemis II crew officially moonbound after successful Translunar Injection burn.
details: Orion executed a critical 5 minute and 55 second TLI burn to break free from Earth's gravitational pull.
Crew is currently performing medical demonstrations, including CPR simulations and space adaptation assessments.
The Waste Management System (toilet) was successfully troubleshot on Day 1 and is fully operational.
Orion is following a free-return trajectory toward the Moon with a lunar flyby expected on April 6.
history.crewStatus: Commander Reid Wiseman, Pilot Victor Glover, and Specialists Christina Koch and Jeremy Hansen are in good health and high spirits.
history.lastEvent: Translunar Injection (TLI) burn completed at 23:49Z on April 2, 2026.
history.nextEvent: Outbound Trajectory Correction (OTC-1) scheduled for April 3 at 22:49Z.

history.currentPhase: Outbound coasting trajectory towards the Moon
history.headline: The Artemis II mission is performing well following the successful completion of the translunar injection burn.
details: Crew is in good health and has successfully acclimated to the space environment.
The Integrity spacecraft is on a precise trajectory toward the Moon.
Operations currently focus on cabin configuration and spacecraft system checkouts.
A minor operational issue with the spacecraft's waste management system was successfully resolved.
history.crewStatus: All four crew members are in good health and have completed their first deep space rest period.
history.lastEvent: Translunar Injection (TLI) burn completed on April 2, 2026.
history.nextEvent: Outbound trajectory correction (OTC) burn on April 3, 2026.
